Biological Scenarios: A Discovery Guide

scenarios.Rmd

clade is a discovery scientific virtual engine for evolutionary biology. Each scenario below implements a tested biological mechanism and asks: what happens next? Every scenario page documents expected dynamics from theory, reports what the model actually produces (with real numbers, not just code), and points toward experiments that go beyond existing theory.

Use the navigation menu above to jump directly to any scenario. The table below maps all 35 scenarios to their biological theme.

Theme 1 — How do traits evolve?

Genetics, heritability, trait architecture, mutation dynamics.

Scenario Key module What it tests
Baseline world core Natural selection on foraging behaviour
Population genetics & heritability body_size_evolution h² from parent-offspring regression
Body size evolution body_size_evolution Allometric cost–benefit under predation
Brain size evolution brain_size_evolution Parental provisioning hypothesis
Stress hypermutation stress_hypermutation Adaptive mutation under starvation

Theme 2 — Ecology and adaptive landscapes

Environment-phenotype feedbacks, spatial structure, niche.

Scenario Key module What it tests
Complex landscape complex_landscape Canopy/shrub/ground habitat partitioning
Dispersal, IFD & spatial sorting dispersal_evolution, spatial_sorting Evolved dispersal and habitat matching
Niche construction niche_construction Shelter building and grass-growth suppression
Seasonal dynamics seasonal_amplitude Resource cycles and life history buffering
Scavenging & carrion scavenging Energy recycling from dead agents

Theme 3 — Social evolution

When does helping pay? Cooperation, signals, and divergence.

Scenario Key module What it tests
Kin selection kin_selection Hamilton’s rule in a spatially explicit model
Cooperative breeding & public goods cooperation, cooperative_breeding Heritable altruism and helper dynamics
Signals & mate choice signal_dims, sexual_selection Signal-toxicity coevolution
Speciation & genetic divergence n_species Ecological speciation via reproductive isolation

Theme 4 — Life history strategies

How life is organised across time and energy.

Scenario Key module What it tests
Parental care parental_care Altricial development and offspring buffering
Mating systems ploidy, sexual_selection Sexual vs asexual reproduction
Life history strategies life_history Semelparous vs iteroparous evolution
Clutch size evolution clutch_size_evolution r/K strategy trade-offs
Parental investment female_investment Quality vs quantity offspring trade-off
Pace-of-life syndromes metabolic_rate_evolution Fast-slow life history continuum

Theme 5 — Species interactions and arms races

Predator-prey, coevolution, disease, mimicry.

Scenario Key module What it tests
Predator-prey dynamics n_predators_init Lotka-Volterra cycles in discrete space
Group defense group_defense Dilution of risk and vigilance
Mimicry & toxicity mimicry, toxicity Batesian/Müllerian mimicry coevolution
SIR disease disease Epidemic dynamics and immune evolution
Predation & neural evolution n_predators_init, brain_type Cognitive arms races

Theme 6 — Learning, plasticity, and cognition

Within-lifetime change interacting with genetic evolution.

Scenario Key module What it tests
Within-lifetime RL rl_mode REINFORCE policy gradient during life
Social learning social_learning Prestige-biased copying
Phenotypic plasticity plasticity_evolution Canalization vs flexibility trade-off
BNN uncertainty canalization epigenetics, brain_type = "bnn" Baldwin Effect via sigma evolution
Cephalopod paradox rl_mode, max_age Short lifespan selects for fast learning

Theme 7 — Discovery experiments

Combinatorial, computational, and open-ended.

Scenario What it tests
Module comparison 14-condition experiment across module combinations
MAP-Elites diversity search Quality-diversity optimisation of parameter space
Kitchen-sink run All modules simultaneously
Evolution of bad science Publication pressure and false discovery rate
Cross-module discovery gallery 6 executed cross-module experiments with multi-seed results

How to use this guide

Each scenario page contains:

  1. What it models — the biological mechanism and key parameters
  2. Example — runnable code (eval = FALSE; requires Julia) and a figure
  3. What we found — real numbers from actual simulation runs
  4. Discovery experiments — three specific experiments that go beyond the baseline, with “Tried it.” results for experiment 1

All code requires Julia. Check availability with julia_is_ready(). The parameter reference for all 200+ specs is at Parameter reference.

library(clade)
if (julia_is_ready()) {
  s <- default_specs()
  e <- run_alife(s)
  plot_run(get_run_data(e))
}

On this page
